title: Use MCP with Hermes
summary: Practical guide for using MCP with Hermes Agent day-to-day: when to use MCP vs built-in tools, step-by-step setup, filtering best practices, WSL2 browser bridging, common patterns (project assistant, GitHub triage, internal API, docs), and troubleshooting by symptom.

cli_commands.purpose: Add an MCP server via CLI
Reload MCP servers and refresh tool list after config changes

config_keys.description: Whitelist of tool names to register
Blacklist of tool names to exclude

slash_commands.purpose: Reload MCP config and refresh available tools

code_examples.caption: GitHub triage with strict whitelist
code_examples.code:
mcp_servers:
  github:
    command: "npx"
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    env:
      GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "***"
    tools:
      include: [list_issues, create_issue, update_issue, search_code]
      prompts: false
      resources: false

code_examples.caption: WSL2 to Windows Chrome bridge
code_examples.code: hermes mcp add chrome-devtools-win --command cmd.exe --args /c npx -y chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --autoConnect --no-usage-statistics

code_examples.caption: Local project assistant
code_examples.code:
mcp_servers:
  fs:
    command: "npx"
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/home/user/project"]
  git:
    command: "uvx"
    args: ["mcp-server-git", "--repository", "/home/user/project"]

gotchas: Don't wait to filter — filter immediately when adding a server that exposes many or dangerous tools
Start Hermes from a Windows-mounted path (/mnt/c/...) when using Windows stdio executables through MCP in WSL2
Prefer include whitelists over exclude blacklists for sensitive systems (financial, customer-facing, destructive)
Use enabled: false to temporarily disable a server without removing its config
Always /reload-mcp after changing include/exclude lists, enabled flags, auth headers, or env vars
WSL2: /browser connect may fail for Windows Chrome — use chrome-devtools-mcp as an MCP server instead
